input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.ItemsPage">

  <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
               IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
      <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <DataTemplate>
          <Grid Padding="10">
            <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
          </Grid>
        </DataTemplate>
      </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

  </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;            // clear so re-selection works
    }
}
```

**Key binding relationships:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Triggers API reload on pull |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Controls spinner; set `false` when done |
| `CollectionView.ItemsSource` | `Items` | The data collection |
| `CollectionView.SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` | Two-way; holds tapped item |
| `CollectionView.SelectionChangedCommand` | `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires navigation on tap |

**Important notes:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the same item can be re-selected on return.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of your refresh — `RefreshView` won't dismiss the spinner automatically.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
